title: Add Collections to Adaptive Orbit Dock (4th item) with Collections screen

requirements.summary: Extend OrbitDock to include a 4th dock item (Collections) with 90° symmetrical distribution while preserving all existing swipe behavior and animations
acceptanceCriteria: Dock renders exactly 4 icons: Upload, Folders, Mission, Collections
Icons are distributed at exactly 90° intervals on the orbit circle
Active icon always appears at the bottom-center position
Swiping left/right rotates the orbit and cycles items without opening any view
Tapping the center (active) icon opens the corresponding feature
No icon overlap at any rotation state
All existing scale/opacity/animation transitions are preserved
Reduced-motion mode works correctly with 4 items

file_operations.description: Add Collections as the 4th dock item. Update the items array to include a Collections entry with id 'collections', label 'Collections', and onTap callback. Adjust the angular distribution logic to space 4 items at exactly 90° intervals (0°, 90°, 180°, 270°) around the orbit circle. Ensure the activeIndex cycling logic accounts for 4 items. Preserve all existing swipe gesture handling, infinite rotation, scale/opacity transitions, and reduced-motion support. The active item must remain at the bottom-center position (270° or equivalent in the coordinate system). Verify the SVG icon rendering for Collections is included inline (see REQ-2).

requirements.summary: Design and implement the Collections SVG icon with a 2×2 grid-of-squares style, warm amber theming, and 60% opacity when inactive
acceptanceCriteria: Collections icon is an inline SVG 2×2 grid of squares with rounded corners
Stroke width is between 2.2 and 2.5px
Icon color is #D97706 in light mode and #FBBF24 in dark mode
Inactive state renders at 60% opacity
Icon aesthetic is consistent with Upload, Folders, and Mission icons

file_operations.description: Add an inline SVG icon for Collections within the OrbitDock component's icon rendering logic. The icon must be a 2×2 grid of four rounded squares (rect elements with rx/ry for rounded corners). Use a stroke width of 2.2–2.5px and no fill. Apply theme-aware colors: #D97706 in light mode and #FBBF24 in dark mode, matching the conditional color logic used for the other icons. Ensure the icon respects the 60% opacity rule when inactive, consistent with the existing opacity transitions for Upload, Folders, and Mission icons. The icon viewBox and dimensions should match the scale of the other dock icons.

requirements.summary: Create CollectionsFullScreenView component that aggregates all uploaded items from UploadContext and React Query without altering the floating bottom-left stack
acceptanceCriteria: Tapping Collections icon when active opens CollectionsFullScreenView
Screen aggregates all uploaded files, notes, and links from existing data sources
Floating bottom-left upload stack is unchanged and still visible when Collections is closed
Screen has a back/close action to return to the main view
Component follows the same full-screen view pattern as FoldersFullScreenView and MissionsFullScreenView

file_operations.description: Create a new full-screen view component that displays all uploaded files, notes, and links as an aggregated collection. The component must accept an onClose callback prop and render a full-screen overlay with a back button in the header. Query all uploaded items by combining data from UploadContext (for in-progress and recently completed uploads) and React Query hooks (useGetAllFiles from useQueries.ts and useGetAllNotes from useNotesQueries.ts). Merge and deduplicate items by id. Render the aggregated list in a 4-column thumbnail grid (see REQ-4 for grid layout specifics). Follow the same structural pattern as FoldersFullScreenView and MissionsFullScreenView: fixed header with title and back button, scrollable content area, and theme-aware styling. Do not modify or interfere with the existing FloatingFileStack or StackFilesFullScreenView components.

requirements.summary: Render all items in CollectionsFullScreenView as a responsive 4-column thumbnail grid with 80×80px cells, 8–12px gaps, 16px edge padding, and lazy loading
acceptanceCriteria: Grid renders 4 columns at 390px screen width
Each cell is 80×80px with 8–12px gap between cells
16px padding on left and right screen edges
Thumbnail corners have 8–10px border-radius
Image/file items show a visual preview thumbnail
Note items show a card with a note/document icon
Link items show a favicon or fallback link icon
Lazy loading is applied so off-screen thumbnails load on scroll

file_operations.description: Implement the thumbnail grid layout within CollectionsFullScreenView. Use CSS Grid with 4 columns (grid-cols-4) and a gap of 8–12px (gap-2 or gap-3). Apply 16px horizontal padding (px-4) to the grid container. Each thumbnail cell must be 80×80px (w-20 h-20) with 8–10px rounded corners (rounded-lg or rounded-xl). For files with image MIME types, render an tag using the file's blob.getDirectURL(). For notes, render a card thumbnail with a document/note icon (reuse or adapt the note icon from existing components). For links, attempt to display a favicon using the link's domain, or fall back to a generic link icon. Implement lazy loading using the loading='lazy' attribute on tags or a lazy-load library like react-intersection-observer to defer off-screen thumbnail rendering until the user scrolls. Ensure touch-friendly tap targets by adding appropriate padding or min-height to each cell.

requirements.summary: Reuse existing file-selection logic (single, multi, select-all, move, delete, share) from GallerySection and StackFilesFullScreenView without creating new logic
acceptanceCriteria: Long-press or tap initiates single-item selection using existing selection state
Multi-select works the same as in GallerySection/StackFilesFullScreenView
Select All selects all items in the Collections grid
Move to Mission opens the existing MoveToMissionDialog
Move to Folder opens the existing SendToFolderDialog
Delete triggers the existing delete mutation/confirmation flow
Share triggers the existing share utility
No new mutation hooks or selection state managers are introduced

file_operations.description: Wire up the existing selection and action logic from GallerySection and StackFilesFullScreenView. Import and use the useStackedFiles hook from frontend/src/hooks/useStackedFiles.ts to manage selected items. Add long-press detection (via touch events or a timer-based approach) to enter selection mode and add items to the stack. Render a selection toolbar at the bottom (similar to GallerySection) when selectedFiles.length > 0, with buttons for Select All, Move to Mission, Move to Folder, Delete, and Share. For Move to Mission, conditionally render <MoveToMissionDialog> with the selected file/note ids. For Move to Folder, conditionally render <SendToFolderDialog>. For Delete, call the existing deleteFiles/deleteNotes mutations from useQueries.ts and useNotesQueries.ts with confirmation. For Share, invoke the existing share utility from frontend/src/utils/externalOpen.ts. Do not create new mutation hooks or duplicate selection state management; strictly reuse the existing patterns.

requirements.summary: Wire CollectionsFullScreenView into HomePage using the same conditional rendering pattern as FoldersFullScreenView and MissionsFullScreenView
acceptanceCriteria: HomePage gains a showCollections boolean state, defaulting to false
OrbitDock receives an onCollectionsTap prop that sets showCollections to true
CollectionsFullScreenView mounts/unmounts based on showCollections
All other HomePage state and rendered components are unaffected
Existing Upload, Folders, and Mission tap callbacks are unchanged

file_operations.description: Add a showCollections boolean state using useState, defaulting to false. Define an onCollectionsTap callback that sets showCollections to true. Pass onCollectionsTap as a new prop to the <OrbitDock> component (which will be wired up in REQ-1). Conditionally render <CollectionsFullScreenView> when showCollections is true, passing an onClose callback that sets showCollections back to false. Follow the exact same conditional rendering and state management pattern used for showFolders/FoldersFullScreenView and showMissions/MissionsFullScreenView. Ensure no other HomePage state, hooks, or component rendering logic is modified. Verify that all existing tap callbacks (onUploadTap, onFoldersTap, onMissionTap) remain unchanged.
